calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-01-2019 Continued To Feb-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 613
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Court Trial/Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 613, Judge TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK S. RAVINANTAPRICHA; CSR PEGGY TSUJIMOTO #5229.

CAMILO ARTIGA-PURCELL, Esq. appeared for and with plaintiff Burness C. Broussard.
Plaintiff was placed under oath and direct examined.
Plaintiff's written declaration was presented to the court.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Confidential exhibits were presented and reviewed in open court, and were returned to counsel
After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant Apprenda, Inc. as follows:
Damages/Principal - $4,476,848.88
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
At plaintiff's request, matter is continued to Feb. 15, 2019 at 9:30 a.m.for further status hearing as to Co-Defendant Kismatic, Inc.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

COOLEY LLP AND ATTY. PATRICK E. GIBBS' MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL.

GRANTED. No opposition filed.

The Court reviews the proposed order submitted to the contest email. Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq. of Artiga-Purcell Law Office, 415.226.7651, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Ara Jabagchourian, Esq. of the Law Offices of Ara Jabagchourian, 650.437.6840, appearing as moving party/counsel for Defendant.

Matter called. Mr. Artiga-Purcell states that he does not contest the Court's tentative ruling and that he is appearing today to request that the Court add language to the proposed order. He requests that Court order the Plaintiff to retain new counsel within 30 days. Discussions are held. At the conclusion of the discussions, Mr. Artiga-Purcell'e request was denied by the Court. Mr. Jabagchourian stated that notice was given to Mr. Patrick O'Reilly.

The Court then entered the following ruling: THE LAW OFFICES OF ARA JABAGCHOURIAN, P.C's NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL is Granted. Order signed. (see order for full details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Set For Trial On Jan-14-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq. 415-226-7662 for Plaintiff Burness Broussard.
Amanda Main, Esq. 650-843-5914 of Cooley for Defendant APPRENDA, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Both sides Argue. Defendant argues Deficiencies. Plaintiff argues two issues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT APPRENDA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF BURNESS C. BROUSSARD'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Apprenda, Inc's demurrer to the first amended complaint's cause of action for tortious interference with prospective economic relations is OVERRULED. Liberally construed, the FAC pleads that plaintiff Broussard had a reasonably probable expectation of economic benefit from his relationships with Kismatic, Inc. and defendant Reilly. This benefit would have been realized through Broussard's receiving a fair share of any deal involving sale of Kismatic assets or stock, and, had it not been for Apprenda's interference, Broussard would not have lost the prospective benefit. California law permits pleading of factually inconsistent theories. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed.(see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, cjordan@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Continued To Apr-25-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Camilo Artiga-Purcell, Esq. 415-226-7651 for Plaintiff Burness Broussard.
Philip Gregory Esq. 650-697-6000 of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy LLP for Plaintiff Burness Broussard.
Patrick Gibbs, Esq. 650-843-5535 of Cooley for Defendant APPRENDA, INC.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues the fifth, eleventh, fifteenth and sixteenth. Defendant responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT APPRENDA, INC.'s DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is Taken under submission.* Defendant submits a proposed order in Open Court.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)
